title: oligosporus capillaris
intro: Oligosporus capillaris, also known as Asperugo procumbens, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Boraginaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Oligosporus capillaris is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
